task_completion.description: Load the InsectSprays dataset into Jamovi.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jamovi window is open and displays the InsectSprays dataset with 'count' and 'spray' columns visible in the data view.

task_completion.description: Perform a Kruskal-Wallis One-Way ANOVA with 'count' as the dependent variable and 'spray' as the grouping variable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jamovi results pane shows a 'Kruskal-Wallis' or 'One-Way ANOVA (Non-parametric)' table indicating 'count' is analyzed by the grouping variable 'spray'.

task_completion.description: Include DSCF pairwise comparisons and epsilon-squared effect size in the analysis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results pane includes an 'ε²' (epsilon-squared) column in the main Kruskal-Wallis results table and a separate table titled 'Pairwise Comparisons - DSCF'.

task_completion.description: Save the Jamovi project as InsectSprays_KruskalWallis.omv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi window title bar shows 'InsectSprays_KruskalWallis.omv' or a save dialog is visible confirming this filename and the /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/ directory.

task_completion.description: Create a text report named kruskal_wallis_report.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or file manager is open showing a file named 'kruskal_wallis_report.txt' in the /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/ directory.

task_completion.description: Document the statistical results and identify the most effective sprays in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report contains the Chi-squared value (~54.69), p-value (<0.001), epsilon-squared (~0.77), and explicitly identifies the most effective sprays (those with the lowest counts).

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Jamovi application interface being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or R/Python scripts executing the analysis.

integrity.description: The results in Jamovi match the expected statistical outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The statistical values in the Jamovi results pane match the expected values (Chi-squared ~54.69, p < 0.001, ε² ~0.77), indicating genuine computation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Kruskal-Wallis test on 'count' by 'spray' yields a Chi-squared value of approximately 54.69 (acceptable range 52.69 - 56.69), df = 5, and a p-value < 0.001. The epsilon-squared effect size is approximately 0.77 (acceptable range 0.67 - 0.87). The text report should include these values and identify the most effective sprays based on the lowest counts.
